message:
chore(beads): close Premium UX Polish epic and remaining features

- Close phage_explorer-s6nf (Data Loading UX)
- Close phage_explorer-taos (Visual Consistency)
- Close phage_explorer-pcn6 (Performance Perception)
- Close phage_explorer-vntd (Premium UX Polish epic)

All 9 child features complete:
- Error States & Empty States
- Mobile Touch UX Overhaul
- Interaction Feedback Enhancement
- Keyboard Navigation Polish
- Accessibility Compliance
- Data Loading UX
- Visual Consistency - CSS System Consolidation
- State Management Fixes
- Performance Perception - Instant Feel

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

{"id":"phage_explorer-necy","content_hash":"8b9f308dd64d87db463b8a63433df9151fff9e6540bb5dadb4e51eed536fc411","title":"Implement amino acid touch HUD with details","description":"","notes":"# Implement Amino Acid Touch HUD with Details ## Problem Statement When users view the amino acid sequence, they see single-letter codes (M, D, L, A, K, etc.) without any context. Non-specialists don't know what these letters mean or what properties each amino acid has. On mobile/touch devices, tapping on an amino acid should display a rich information overlay (HUD = Heads-Up Display) for the duration of the touch. ## User Story > As a biology student viewing a phage protein sequence, > When I tap and hold on any amino acid letter, > I want to see detailed information about that amino acid, > So that I can understand its properties and role in the protein. ## HUD Content Requirements The HUD should display the following information for each amino acid: ### Basic Information - **Full Name**: e.g., "Methionine" (not just "Met" or "M") - **3-Letter Code**: e.g., "Met" - **1-Letter Code**: e.g., "M" ### Physical Properties - **Molecular Weight**: e.g., "149.21 g/mol" - **Molecular Formula**: e.g., "C₅H₁₁NO₂S" - **pI (Isoelectric Point)**: e.g., "5.74" ### Chemical Classification - **Type**: Hydrophobic / Polar / Acidic / Basic / Special - **Charge at pH 7**: Neutral / Positive / Negative - **Aromaticity**: Yes / No - **Contains Sulfur**: Yes / No ### Biological Context - **Essential**: Yes / No (for humans) - If essential: "Must be obtained from diet" - If non-essential: "Can be synthesized by the body" - **Energy to Synthesize**: ATP equivalents (e.g., "~31 ATP") ### Genetic Encoding - **Codon Count**: e.g., "1 codon" (unique) or "6 codons" (degenerate) - **Codons**: List of all codons encoding this AA - e.g., "AUG (also start codon)" - e.g., "UUU, UUC" for Phenylalanine ## Visual Design ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐ │ ┌───┐ │ │ │ M │ METHIONINE │ │ └───┘ Met · Sulfur-containing │ ├─────────────────────────────────────┤ │ Molecular Weight 149.21 g/mol │ │ Formula C₅H₁₁NO₂S │ │ Classification Hydrophobic │ ├─────────────────────────────────────┤ │ Essential Amino Acid │ │ Must be obtained from diet │ │ Synthesis Cost: ~31 ATP │ ├─────────────────────────────────────┤ │ Encoded by: AUG │ │ (Also serves as START codon) │ │ Codon Uniqueness: 1/1 (unique) │ └─────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ## Technical Implementation ### Data Structure ```typescript interface AminoAcidInfo { code1: string; // M code3: string; // Met name: string; // Methionine formula: string; // C5H11NO2S molecularWeight: number; pI: number; classification: 'hydrophobic' | 'polar' | 'acidic' | 'basic' | 'special'; chargeAtPh7: 'neutral' | 'positive' | 'negative'; isAromatic: boolean; containsSulfur: boolean; isEssential: boolean; synthesisAtp: number; codons: string[]; specialNotes?: string; // e.g., "Start codon" for Met } const AMINO_ACID_DATABASE: Record = { M: { code1: 'M', code3: 'Met', name: 'Methionine', formula: 'C₅H₁₁NO₂S', molecularWeight: 149.21, pI: 5.74, classification: 'hydrophobic', chargeAtPh7: 'neutral', isAromatic: false, containsSulfur: true, isEssential: true, synthesisAtp: 31, codons: ['AUG'], specialNotes: 'Also serves as the universal START codon', }, // ... all 20 amino acids }; ``` ### Touch Event Handling ```typescript function handleTouchStart(event: TouchEvent, position: number) { const aminoAcid = getAminoAcidAtPosition(position); if (!aminoAcid) return; // Show HUD at touch position showAminoAcidHUD(aminoAcid, { x: event.touches[0].clientX, y: event.touches[0].clientY, }); } function handleTouchEnd() { hideAminoAcidHUD(); } ``` ### Position Detection Need to map touch coordinates to sequence position: ```typescript function getAminoAcidAtPosition( touchX: number, touchY: number, gridLayout: GridLayout ): string | null { const cellIndex = gridLayout.getCellAtPoint(touchX, touchY); if (cellIndex === null) return null; // Account for reading frame const codonIndex = Math.floor(cellIndex / 3); const aaSequence = translateSequence(dnaSequence, readingFrame); return aaSequence[codonIndex] ?? null; } ``` ## HUD Component ```tsx function AminoAcidHUD({ aminoAcid, position, visible }: Props) { if (!visible || !aminoAcid) return null; const info = AMINO_ACID_DATABASE[aminoAcid]; return (
{info.code1}
{info.name}
{info.code3} · {info.classification}
{/* ... rest of HUD content */}
); } ``` ## Files to Create/Modify 1. **New:** `/packages/core/src/data/amino-acid-database.ts` - Complete database of all 20 amino acids 2. **New:** `/packages/web/src/components/AminoAcidHUD.tsx` - HUD component with styling 3. **Modify:** `/packages/web/src/components/SequenceView.tsx` - Add touch event handlers for HUD 4. **Modify:** `/packages/web/src/hooks/useSequenceGrid.ts` - Add position-to-amino-acid mapping ## Testing - [ ] Tap and hold shows HUD - [ ] Release dismisses HUD - [ ] All 20 amino acids have complete data - [ ] HUD positions correctly (above finger, not obscured) - [ ] Works on iOS Safari and Android Chrome - [ ] Smooth show/hide animation - [ ] Data is scientifically accurate ## Definition of Done - [ ] Touch HUD functional on mobile devices - [ ] All 20 amino acids have complete information - [ ] HUD is visually polished and readable - [ ] Scientific accuracy verified - [ ] No interference with normal scrolling","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:25:52.966266-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-09T11:48:13.199534-05:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-necy","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-8mgm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:26:24.161822-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"phage_explorer-nm9","content_hash":"61a6e37d0fd98d1c0e0b50b4bd794a8cd88f1d14f9ed5b69aa1ed23ea90c0dde","title":"Advanced: High-Resolution K-mer Anomaly Cartography","description":"Map localized k-mer rarity via z-scores vs reference corpus. Sliding window with Welford variance + LOF. Fuse with GC skew and coding density. Scrolling heat-strip under sequence grid; hotspots pulse; side panel shows top k-mers and genes. Flags HGT/AMG/defense islands. Novelty: Medium-high.","notes":"## HIGH-RESOLUTION K-MER ANOMALY CARTOGRAPHY **Full Implementation from Roadmap** ### Concept Map localized k-mer rarity/novelty (z-scores vs a reference corpus) to expose atypical islands (HGT, AMGs, anti-defense loci) and couple with GC/AT skew and coding density. ### How to Build **Background Model:** - Global corpus k-mer frequencies (k=3/5/7/9) precomputed - Store means/variances per k - Sources: all known phage genomes **Sliding Windows:** - Rust+WASM streaming k-mer counter - Welford's online variance algorithm - Per-window z-scores and LOF (Local Outlier Factor) - Cache per phage in SQLite **Composite Score:** ``` anomaly = w₁×z_kmer + w₂×GC_skew + w₃×(1-coding_density) ``` - Weights configurable by user - Higher score = more anomalous **Drill-Down:** - Link anomalous windows to nearby genes - Show top contributing k-mers - Motif and function predictions ### TUI Features - Scrolling heat-strip under sequence grid - Hotspots pulse/glow - Press 'enter' to open side panel with: - Top-k unusual k-mers - Nearby genes - Putative function - Anomaly threshold slider ### Why It's Good - Flags HGT, defense, AMG islands that annotation misses - Prioritizes 'what's weird here?' - Alignment-free detection - Compositional biology insights ### Implementation Stack - Rust+WASM sliding k-mer stats - TS/Ink heat-strip visualization - SQLite window cache - Optional LOF in Rust with kd-tree Novelty: 8/10, Pedagogical: 8/10, Wow Factor: 8/10","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T00:17:45.217066-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T13:27:18.933998-05:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-nm9","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-ojb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T00:20:52.603582-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"phage_explorer-nmi","content_hash":"c9c1490c5fb3fc68c95150ffc4b48795e759335b7e0479df2a7fd9615aa88cc3","title":"[WEB-P1] Create offline.html fallback page","description":"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:58:29.441559-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:53:55.445034-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T17:53:55.445034-05:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-nmi","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-fdq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:04:45.297385-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} @@ -363,7 +363,7 @@ {"id":"phage_explorer-owsv","content_hash":"e2bc20c7af49357a3b5af6af186e20118bb16d6ec41f9aec50c9cdc0b6d555d7","title":"Task: Create useBeginnerMode hook","description":"Create packages/web/src/education/hooks/useBeginnerMode.ts with: isEnabled (boolean), toggle(), showContextFor(topic), hasCompletedModule(id), hasCompletedTour(id). Should be memoized and performant since many components will use it.","notes":"Hook implemented in packages/web/src/education/hooks/useBeginnerMode.ts providing isEnabled/toggle/showContextFor/hasCompletedModule/hasCompletedTour.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:43:07.701492-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T13:58:24.674795465-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T13:58:24.674800264-06:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-owsv","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-chdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:43:07.70295-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"phage_explorer-owy","content_hash":"310f5ff4aa1a73c2dfb4d6d668fb4b7f1669e8e8ce567afdcfa904a2a60c08e0","title":"Layer 2: Host Range Predictor (CRISPR Spacer Matching)","description":"Fuse CRISPR spacer hits with receptor-binding motif classifiers. Score per host lineage with evidence (spacer alignments, RBP motif hits). MinHash/LSH index for spacers + HMMER for RBP families. Bayesian fusion scoring. Evidence table per host with strict/permissive thresholds, host cloud sized by probability. Novelty: High, Pedagogical: High.","notes":"## HOST-RANGE INFERENCE VIA CRISPR SPACERS + RECEPTOR MOTIFS **Full Implementation from Roadmap** ### Concept Fuse CRISPR spacer hits with receptor-binding motif classifiers to predict host range. Score per host lineage and display evidence. ### How to Build **Spacer DB Ingest:** - Parse spacer FASTAs/TSVs from CRISPRdb, IMG/VR, CRISPR-Cas++ - Build MinHash/LSH index (Rust+WASM) for fast lookup - Fall back to banded alignments for top hits - Store spacer→host mappings with confidence **Motif/RBP Typing:** - HMMER precompute for RBP families (beta-helix, depolymerase, etc.) - TS classifier on receptor-binding domain signatures - Link RBP domains to known receptors (LPS, OmpA, FhuA, etc.) **Fusion Scoring:** ``` log P(host | spacer, motif) = w₁ × spacer_score + w₂ × motif_score + prior Confidence = σ(log_odds) ``` **Evidence Types:** - Spacer alignments with mismatches - RBP domain hits with E-values - Known receptor associations ### TUI Features - Host leaderboard with confidence bars - Press host to see spacer hit list and RBP motif hits - 'Host cloud' ASCII plot sized by probability - Toggle 'strict vs permissive' thresholds - Evidence drilldown per candidate ### Why It's Good - Combines complementary evidence types - Tightens weak host predictions - Points to mechanisms (which receptor, which spacer) - Guides experimental validation ### Implementation Stack - Rust+WASM LSH for spacer queries - HMMER precompute for RBP domains - Fusion/scoring in TS - SQLite cache for hits and scores - Ink UI tables and plots Novelty: 9/10, Pedagogical: 9/10, Wow Factor: 8/10","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:12:45.734-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-08T20:58:17.503429-05:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-owy","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-54g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:12:45.735205-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-owy","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-2g5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:39:32.636107-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} Vision Transform Phage Explorer from a terminal application into a browser-native experience that *amplifies* the cyberpunk aesthetic through GPU-accelerated visuals, while preserving—and enhancing—the keyboard-driven immediacy that makes the TUI feel alive. ## Strategic Goals 1. **Preserve** the keyboard-first, low-latency interaction model 2. **Amplify** the cyberpunk aesthetic with GPU-accelerated effects (phosphor glow, scanlines, chromatic aberration) 3. **Extend** capabilities with web-only features (collaboration, video export, offline) 4. **Maintain** a single codebase for core logic and computation via shared packages 5. **Deploy** seamlessly on Vercel with no backend infrastructure ## Technical Approach - **Framework**: React 19 + Vite + Bun - **State**: Zustand (shared with TUI) - **Rendering**: Canvas 2D + WebGL for effects + WebGPU (experimental) - **Database**: sql.js (SQLite WASM) with IndexedDB caching - **Offline**: Service Worker with Workbox - **Deployment**: Vercel static hosting + edge functions ## Architecture Overview ``` packages/ ├── core/ # Shared (existing) ├── state/ # Shared (existing) + web persistence layer ├── wasm-compute/ # Shared (existing) ├── db-runtime/ # Extended with browser adapter ├── web/ # NEW: Web application │ ├── src/ │ │ ├── app/ # React app structure │ │ ├── components/ │ │ ├── rendering/ # Canvas/WebGL renderers │ │ ├── workers/ # Web Workers │ │ └── service-worker/ └── tui/ # Existing (preserved) ``` ## Design Principles 1. **Terminal Authenticity, Not Simulation** - We adopt the aesthetic vocabulary (monospace, scanlines, phosphor glow) while exploiting web's superpowers 2. **Keyboard-First, Mouse-Aware** - Every action is keyboard-accessible, mouse/touch are accelerators 3. **Instant Feedback** - <16ms visual feedback, <50ms action completion, <150ms complex computations 4. **Progressive Disclosure** - Novice→Intermediate→Power user journey 5. **Offline-First** - Complete functionality after first load ## Success Metrics - First Contentful Paint: <1.0s - Time to Interactive: <2.0s - Keypress to Paint: <16ms - 60fps scrolling on 100k+ base sequences - 100% hotkey parity with TUI - 100% overlay parity with TUI - Full offline functionality ## Phased Execution - **Phase 0**: Foundation (scaffold, themes, keyboard) - **Phase 1**: Data Layer (sql.js, caching, offline) - **Phase 2**: Core Visualization (canvas sequence grid, gene map, sparklines) - **Phase 3**: Overlays & Panels (command palette, analysis menu, overlays) - **Phase 4**: Simulations (worker orchestration, all sims) - **Phase 5**: Advanced Features (heavy analysis, WebGPU, collaboration) - **Phase 6**: Polish & Launch (performance, accessibility, onboarding) ## References - See PLAN_TO_DEPLOY_PHAGE_EXPLORER_AS_WEBAPP.md for full technical specifications","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:54:03.040434-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-08T00:09:54.184757-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-08T00:09:54.184757-05:00","source_repo":"."} @@ -412,7 +412,7 @@ {"id":"phage_explorer-t9j","content_hash":"d03605f6a4af27cee317f5e0ca093523d6acce32c17b9782baa77f6ea8c28238","title":"Advanced: Structural Epitope Clash Map","description":"## Structural Epitope Clash Map (Tail Fiber Host Range Analyzer) ### Concept The tail fiber is the phage's MOLECULAR KEY — its receptor-binding domain (RBD) determines host specificity. This feature combines: 1. **Structural analysis**: ΔΔG alanine scans and electrostatics on predicted structures 2. **Modular domain detection**: Identify N-anchor, shaft, and hypervariable RBD regions 3. **Entropy-based variability mapping**: Find conserved vs rapidly-evolving positions 4. **Host range prediction**: Infer likely hosts from RBD sequence features ### Mathematical Foundations **Shannon entropy for per-position variability:** H(i) = -Σ p(a) × log₂(p(a)) for each alignment position - H(i) = 0: Perfectly conserved (functional constraint) - H(i) = 4.3: Maximum entropy (20 AA, no constraint) Entropy profile reveals low entropy in anchor/shaft (structural), high in RBD (host-range variability) **Domain boundary detection:** Tail fibers have conserved modular structure: [N-anchor] — [Shaft repeats] — [RBD] Detect via: - Entropy gradient peaks (sudden variability change) - Hydropathy profile transitions - Secondary structure predictions (α-helix shaft → β-sheet RBD) - Recombination breakpoint detection **Structural stability (ΔΔG):** ΔΔG = ΔG(wild-type) - ΔG(alanine mutant) - High ΔΔG = structurally important (core) - Low ΔΔG = surface-exposed, tolerant to mutation - Negative ΔΔG = destabilizing, likely under selection **Host range prediction features:** For ML model: k-mer frequencies (k=3,4,5), AA composition, predicted secondary structure, isoelectric point, molecular weight, specific motifs (carbohydrate-binding, Ig-like folds) ### Implementation - entropyProfile(alignment) → Float64Array of per-position entropy - detectDomainBoundaries(entropy, hydropathy, secondaryStructure) → DomainBoundary[] - predictHostRange(rbdSequence, knownRBDs) → HostPrediction[] with confidence - analyzeStructuralEpitopes(pdbId, sequence) → StructuralRisk[] (ΔΔG, surface area, charge) ### TUI Visualization (Multi-Layer) - Domain structure bar: N-anchor | Shaft | RBD with conservation coloring - Entropy profile sparkline (low in anchor, high in RBD) - Structural risk profile (ΔΔG instability) - Host range prediction table with confidence bars - Chimera suggestion: "Swap RBD (aa 850-1132) with P22 gpJ → Predicted new host: Salmonella" - 3D ASCII model with epitope highlighting (charge/risk mode toggle) ### Pedagogical Value: 9/10 Teaches receptor-ligand biology, protein modularity, information theory (Shannon entropy), structural biology (surface exposure, electrostatics), evolution of specificity (arms race in molecular detail). ### External Dependencies - AlphaFold DB or ESMFold for structure prediction - FoldX (or ML surrogate) for ΔΔG calculations - APBS for electrostatics ### Priority: P4 (highest complexity)","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:21:06.440127-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-06T23:31:50.332681-05:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-t9j","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-54g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:21:06.441895-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"phage_explorer-vru3","content_hash":"ce23fee61cc8443c5e7c20813a127d4d1eab7eb30158beec1f307738695f298d","title":"Feature: 3D Rendering Overhaul","description":"","notes":"# Feature: 3D Rendering Overhaul ## Executive Summary A comprehensive overhaul of the 3D molecular structure viewer to address critical usability and visual quality issues. ## Current Problems 1. **Zoom-to-Extents Broken**: Molecules don't auto-fit to viewport (THIRD TIME REQUESTED) 2. **Poor Atom Coloring**: Ball-and-stick mode uses colors that are hard to distinguish 3. **Redundant Render Mode**: Cartoon mode is nearly identical to Ribbon 4. **Missing Phages**: Some catalog phages may not appear with 3D models ## Sub-Tasks ### P0 - Critical - `phage_explorer-de4o`: Fix zoom-to-extents auto-fit ### P1 - High Priority - `phage_explorer-qm08`: Implement CPK element coloring - `phage_explorer-z49z`: Verify all phages have 3D models ### P2 - Normal Priority - `phage_explorer-xkxf`: Remove redundant Cartoon mode - `phage_explorer-k3l5`: Add functional group visualization ## Technical Context **File Locations:** - Model viewer: `/packages/web/src/components/Model3DView.tsx` - Structure loader: `/packages/web/src/visualization/structure-loader.ts` - Phage catalog: `/packages/data-pipeline/src/phage-catalog.ts` **Key Technologies:** - Three.js for WebGL rendering - React Query for structure fetching - RCSB PDB for structure data ## Success Criteria - [ ] All molecules auto-fit to viewport on load - [ ] Atoms clearly distinguishable by element type - [ ] Only 3 render modes (Ball-Stick, Ribbon, Surface) - [ ] All 12 catalog phages have working 3D models - [ ] Functional groups highlighted in complex structures ## Definition of Done All sub-tasks completed and tested on multiple phages across different device sizes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:25:34.423964-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-09T11:34:11.514737-05:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-vru3","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-jjq5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:26:24.298042-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"phage_explorer-vs9k","content_hash":"c88d05fa88fc226763f3d80921c80d361e93499d450fdd81fc0c516f0a9fe08d","title":"Pillar 5: Context-Aware Help System","description":"## Smart Explanations That Appear When Relevant ### Philosophy Instead of requiring users to seek out help, context-aware explanations appear automatically based on what the user is viewing. This creates a 'just-in-time learning' experience. ### How It Works 1. **InfoButton Component** - Small [?] icons placed next to technical displays 2. **ContextPanel Component** - Slides in with relevant explanation 3. **useContextHelp Hook** - Determines what explanation to show based on current view ### Context Triggers **When viewing DNA sequence:** - What are these letters? (A, T, G, C) - Why are some colored differently? - What does the position number mean? **When viewing amino acids:** - How did DNA become amino acids? - What do the colors represent? - What is a reading frame? **When viewing genes:** - What is a gene/ORF? - What do start/end positions mean? - What is a locus tag? **When using analysis overlays:** - GC Skew: Why does GC content vary? What does skew indicate? - Complexity: What is sequence entropy? Why does it matter? - Repeats: What are direct/inverted repeats? Why important? - HGT: What is horizontal gene transfer? - CRISPR: What is CRISPR? How do spacers work? - Synteny: What is gene order conservation? - Dot Plot: How do I read this matrix? ### Implementation **ContextPanel Component:** - Slides in from right side - Title, explanation text, diagrams - Links to: glossary terms, full module, visualizer - Dismiss button, auto-dismiss on navigation **InfoButton Component:** - Consistent [?] icon style - Hover preview (short), click for full panel - Only visible in Beginner Mode **ContextProvider:** - Wraps app, tracks current context - Provides `showContext(topicId)` function - Manages panel open/close state ### Content Structure Each context help entry has: - `id`: unique identifier - `title`: what is this? - `shortExplanation`: 1-2 sentences - `fullExplanation`: detailed (2-3 paragraphs) - `glossaryTerms`: linked terms - `relatedModule`: link to foundational module - `visualizer`: optional interactive element ### Files to Create - packages/web/src/education/context/ContextPanel.tsx - packages/web/src/education/context/ContextProvider.tsx - packages/web/src/education/components/InfoButton.tsx - packages/web/src/education/context/topics/*.ts (content files) - packages/web/src/education/hooks/useContextHelp.ts ### Integration Points - SequenceView: add info buttons for DNA/AA display - GeneMapCanvas: add info for gene annotations - Each overlay: add context explanation header - ComparisonOverlay: explain metrics ### Why This Pillar Matters This is the 'magic' of Beginner Mode—help appears exactly when needed without the user having to search. It transforms the app from 'tool for experts' to 'learning environment for everyone'.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:41:56.750332-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-09T11:41:56.750332-05:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-vs9k","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-ty1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:41:56.751579-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-vs9k","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-0weo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:41:56.753088-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
